Javelin testing initiative · Wave 1 recruitment

Test Attack the editor that never guesses.

Javelin previews every edit, refuses ambiguity, and never rewrites your work. Project Attack-Agents exists to find the one case where that isn't true — before a lawyer, a doctor, or a student does. Your mission: find it first.

File 01 · The Doctrine

Ordinary beta testers use the app.
Attack-Agents hunt it.

“The best way to build a trustworthy product is to let people try to break it.”

● What Javelin claims

  • Every edit is previewed as a diff and applied only on your approval.
  • Ambiguity is refused — with the count, and the fix spelled out.
  • Chains are atomic — one failing step rolls back everything.
  • Nothing is rewritten — no autocorrect, no AI prose, no surprises.
  • Everything runs on-device — documents never leave your machine.

● What agents do

  • Try unusual commands and phrasing Javelin's parser never saw coming.
  • Chain commands with then and attack the rollback.
  • Bring real-world documents — contracts, notes, syllabi, data files.
  • Deliberately attempt to confuse it, then grade the refusal.
  • Verify the result matches the claim — word for word.

● What counts as a kill

  • Parser mistakes — a clear command read wrong.
  • Execution bugs — the edit lands in the wrong place.
  • Validation gaps — a refusal that should have been an edit, or vice-versa.
  • UI failures — freezes, broken rendering, lost selections.
  • Performance — anything that makes a big document crawl.

PRIORITY TARGET — the false success: Javelin reports “done” but the document says otherwise. This is the highest-value kill in the program. One confirmed false success outranks ten cosmetic finds.

File 02 · The Attack Range

The full Javelin, live on this page.
No download. No mercy expected.

This is not a mockup — it is the real Javelin engine and the real interface, the same code the desktop app runs, armed right here in your browser. A briefing document is preloaded. Everything is fair game.

Range cold — press Arm Report a finding →

Beyond this line, the editor is live.

Commands, chains, tables, images, redline, undo — all real. If you break something, that's the point. Reset puts the briefing document back.

Web build notes: Open/Save use your browser's picker and downloads. PDF export opens the print dialog — choose “Save as PDF”.

Images: attach from disk or paste into a table cell — they embed into the document as data, private to your browser.

Talk to it: plain English works — “make the deadline red and underline it”. Javelin translates offline (a deterministic phrasebook, not an AI service) and shows Understood as: … with the exact command before anything runs. The translator is a target too.

Same code: engine and interface are identical to the desktop build, so a kill here is a kill there. OS-level targets (file dialogs, installer) need the desktop app — coming below.

File 03 · The Speed

It answers before you finish blinking.

Here is the real deal about Javelin: it is fast. Everything runs on your device — no server, no AI round-trip, no network to wait on. Every command is a plain local computation over text, so the preview appears the instant you press Enter. Most edits finish in well under a millisecond.

<1ms
a typical edit on an everyday document
~4ms
find-and-replace across a 3,000-line contract
~40ms
a full legal redline of a 200 KB document
<0.2s
the slowest thing we could measure — a whole-document compare on a ~250-page file

Don’t take our word for it.

Run the real Javelin engine on a large generated document, right here. Nothing is uploaded — the clock runs on your machine, in your browser.

File 06 · Divisions

Every profession attacks differently.
Pick your division.

Each division carries missions drawn from its real workload. Run them verbatim, then improvise — the best kills come from documents only you would think to bring.

Division 01

Legal

Lawyers · paralegals · compliance

  1. Rename a party across a whole agreement — replace all "Contractor" with "Consultant" — then hunt for the one it missed.
  2. Track changes, reject them out of order, and check the rollback doesn't lie.
  3. Compare your draft against a returned version; audit the blackline word by word.
Division 02

Medical

Doctors · nurses · clinical staff

  1. Put dosages in a table; edit around them and verify no command ever touches the wrong cell.
  2. Chain corrections with then — if one step fails, confirm nothing at all changed.
  3. uppercase a heading that carries bold or color — formatting must survive intact.
Division 03

Faculty

Lecturers · teachers · TAs

  1. Build a TOC, reorder sections, run update toc — does it still match reality?
  2. Hand it a mangled reading list and demand renumber lists.
  3. Export the formatted syllabus to PDF and compare it against the page, line by line.
Division 04

Research

Researchers · analysts · writers

  1. move paragraph 3 after paragraph 5 — count the paragraphs yourself first, then check its math.
  2. Insert figures by quoting the sentence they belong to; measure the placement.
  3. Sort your references, then undo — is the restore byte-exact?
Division 05

Office Ops

Admins · managers · coordinators

  1. Open a CSV, a log, a UTF-16 Notepad file — nothing may silently convert or mangle.
  2. Fill a status table in place; paste a screenshot straight into a cell.
  3. count and find before a mass replace — then verify its numbers against yours.
Division 06

Students

Undergrads · postgrads · note-takers

  1. Reformat messy lecture notes: bullets, headings, highlights — at speed.
  2. Give a deliberately ambiguous order and grade the refusal message it sends back.
  3. Interleave typing with commands, then try to break the undo chain.

No division fits? The Free Company takes anyone willing to stress-test. Bring any document you actually work with and make Javelin flinch.

File 07 · Report a Kill

Found a crack? File the report.

A confirmed kill needs four things: what you typed, what Javelin said, what actually happened, and what you expected. Fill them in and hit send — the report goes straight to the Javelin team, and a confirmed kill is credited to you in the next release notes.

CRITICAL Javelin reports success but the document says otherwise. MAJOR Crash, data loss, or a refusal/edit that misreads a clear command. MINOR Cosmetic or UI issues, awkward messages, sluggishness.
Field report
Fill in the form and press “Send field report”. It goes straight to the Javelin team, and a copy appears here.
✉ Send by email instead

Wave 0 is closed. The scoreboard stands.

Before recruitment opened, Javelin was attacked internally — Wave 0. These are its honest numbers. Wave 1 is where your name enters the record.

defects found & neutralized in Wave 0

automated tests now standing guard

of edits previewed before applying — by architecture

Confirmed kills — Wave 0 (selection)

IDDefectClassStatus
W0-01Color and size reported “applied” while the page never changedFalse successNEUTRALIZED
W0-02Filling a freshly inserted table froze the whole appPerformanceNEUTRALIZED
W0-03Case commands corrupted bold/color markup around the targetExecutionNEUTRALIZED
W0-04Italic on bold text left literal * characters on the pageExecutionNEUTRALIZED
W0-05“Insert image after line 12” landed the image before line 10ExecutionNEUTRALIZED
W0-06UTF-16 files from Windows Notepad refused as “binary”ValidationNEUTRALIZED
W0-07“remove font from …” crashed the engine bridgeExecutionNEUTRALIZED
W0-08Image resize behaved as if pinned at the top-left cornerUINEUTRALIZED
W0-09Selection lost after every command in Document viewUINEUTRALIZED
W0-10“after paragraph 3” counted headings and tables as paragraphsParserNEUTRALIZED

Full Wave-0 record ships with the release notes. Wave-1 kills are credited to the reporting agent by name or alias.

v0.6.4 — currentAll text file types open and save with their exact encoding (UTF-8, UTF-16, BOMs) · any-edge image resize · font styles · remove controls for tables and images.
v0.6.xDocument-first views (Document / Source / Redline) · live tables editable in place · drag-resize for columns and images · sentence and paragraph anchors for inserts · PDF export to a location you choose.
v0.5.xThe deterministic core: preview-first edits, refusal on ambiguity, atomic chains, tracked changes with legal redline, compare against a file on disk.

The desktop build

The Windows installer is in final assembly. It adds the OS-level targets the web range can't reach: native file dialogs, drag-a-file-onto-the-app, “Open with…”, and true on-device PDF export. Until it lands, the Attack Range above is the front line — same engine, same kills.

In assembly Attack in the browser instead

File 09 · From the same bench

The maker of Javelin also built Lunar — a fully offline study assistant.

Same principle, different job. Point Lunar at your slides and it teaches them back to you in a hologram classroom — no accounts, no uploads, nothing leaves your machine. If you liked that Javelin runs entirely on your device, you'll feel right at home.

  • A hologram classroom that teaches your slides to you. Drop in a deck; Lunar turns it into a lesson you can actually sit through.
  • 100% offline. No sign-up, no cloud, no telemetry — it runs on your own machine, exactly like Javelin.
  • Built for students who'd rather not hand their notes to someone else's server.
⬇  Get Lunar See how it works →
